title: podman-system-service: support sighup reload
body:
Long-running processes are commonly reloading their configs after receiving sighub. Since `podman-system-service` may be such a long-running process, it could be worth supporting such reloads. Configs to consider:
* containers.conf
* storage.conf
* registries.conf (+ registries.conf.d)
* ...?

Basically a similar behaviour as cri-o.

@baude @jwhonce @mheon @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat WDYT?

comments.body: This could be as simple as stopping + recreating our `libpod.Runtime`, though I'm not sure if that will be enough - some of our config logic is wired as `sync.Once` IIRC.
Initial look suggests that a lot of rootless paths are `sync.Once`'d. And not much else. This might end up being not that bad.

containers.conf is sync.Onced.  But that does not mean the global data could not be substituted for by calling another function.  The `podman system service` is not supposed to be a long running service in general, but I guess could end up that way if under heavy load.
In a perfect world, we could watch for the config file changes with INotify and reload automatically.  But this could become difficult with lots of config files.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@QiWang19 Could you add an interface to containers/common to reload configuration.
